project.name: localpaas-dashboard
project.description: LocalPaaS Dashboard is a Vite-powered React TypeScript frontend organized around feature modules, shared UI primitives, and an infrastructure API/data layer. It uses generated API clients, Zod validation, TanStack Query, React Router, and dashboard-specific module conventions. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

layers.name: Project Configuration And Documentation
layers.description: Repository-level documentation, agent rules, package/build configuration, and setup scripts that govern how the dashboard is developed.

layers.name: Application Shell And Routing
layers.description: The React/Vite boot path, application shell, app-level providers, route wrappers, and global application wiring.

layers.name: Authentication
layers.description: Authentication pages, forms, API access, context, routes, and supporting auth domain/data code.

layers.name: Domain Feature Modules
layers.description: Feature-owned LocalPaaS modules for projects, settings, cluster, sources, user management, system settings, and related dashboard workflows.

layers.name: Shared UI System
layers.description: Reusable UI primitives, visual components, global styling, assets, and common presentation building blocks.

layers.name: Infrastructure API Layer
layers.description: Infrastructure services, HTTP clients, validation, API utilities, data primitives, device services, and framework-level adapters.

layers.name: Utilities Workers And Runtime Config
layers.description: Shared utilities, runtime config helpers, workers, hooks, constants, stores, and cross-cutting frontend support code.

tour.title: Project Orientation
tour.description: Start with the README and development guidelines to understand that this is a Vite React TypeScript dashboard with LocalPaaS-specific module conventions.

tour.title: Application Boot Path
tour.description: Follow the HTML host, Vite entry point, and App component to see how React mounts and hands control to the dashboard shell.

tour.title: Routes And Navigation
tour.description: Review the shared route constants and navigation wrappers that keep module navigation consistent across the application.

tour.title: Authentication Flow
tour.description: Inspect the sign-in route and authentication API hook to see how auth UI, forms, API calls, and state connect.

tour.title: Feature Module Pattern
tour.description: Use the projects module as the representative feature slice: routes present UI, API services talk to the backend, and domain/data folders hold module-owned behavior.

tour.title: Settings Tables And Dialogs
tour.description: Settings demonstrates the repeated operational-dashboard pattern of table definitions, reusable cells, dialogs, and module-scoped shared components.

tour.title: Infrastructure API Layer
tour.description: The infrastructure layer owns HTTP client construction, BaseApi behavior, query builders, interceptors, validation helpers, and error handling.

tour.title: Shared UI Primitives
tour.description: Review the shared UI primitives and table component that feature modules compose into dense dashboard screens.

tour.title: Permissions And Shared Application Utilities
tour.description: Shared application permissions, environment helpers, and stores provide cross-cutting behavior used by multiple modules.

tour.title: Utilities And Workers
tour.description: Finish with generic utility and worker code that supports formatting, background image conversion, and common reusable behavior outside feature ownership.
